stem: Triangle PQR is a right triangle with the right angle at R. If sin(Q) = 8/17 and side PQ has length 34, what is the length of side QR?
rationale: In a right triangle, sin of an acute angle equals the opposite side over the hypotenuse. PQ is the hypotenuse (opposite the right angle). The side opposite angle Q is PR, so sin(Q) = PR/PQ = 8/17. With PQ = 34, PR = 34 · 8/17 = 16. By the Pythagorean theorem, QR2 = PQ2 − PR2 = 1156 − 256 = 900, so QR = 30.
